title: Writing - Part 2: Exercise 4
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 220-260 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt: You have been asked to write a report for the local council on the success of a new 'Green City' initiative, which includes new parks and bike lanes. Describe the impact on the community and suggest further improvements.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Evaluation of the 'Green City' Initiative

Introduction
The purpose of this report is to assess the impact of the 'Green City' initiative recently implemented in our district. This initiative involved the creation of three new urban parks and the expansion of the cycle lane network. The findings are based on a series of interviews with local residents.

Impact on the Community
The response from the public has been overwhelmingly positive. The new parks have provided much-needed recreational space, leading to a noticeable increase in outdoor activities among families and the elderly. Furthermore, the expansion of cycle lanes has encouraged a significant number of residents to commute by bike. Recent traffic data suggests a 12% reduction in car usage during peak hours in the city centre, which has positively affected air quality.

Issues Encountered
Despite its success, some residents have raised concerns. The primary issue is the lack of adequate lighting in the new parks after sunset, which has led to safety concerns during winter months. Additionally, some cycle lanes terminate abruptly, creating confusion and potential hazards for less experienced cyclists.

Recommendations
To build on this success, I suggest the following:
- Installation of solar-powered LED lighting throughout all new park areas.
- Development of a more cohesive cycle map to ensure all lanes are interconnected.
- Introduction of a public bike-sharing scheme to encourage those who do not own a bicycle to participate.

Conclusion
Overall, the 'Green City' initiative has significantly enhanced the quality of life in our district. Addressing the safety and connectivity issues mentioned will ensure its long-term viability.

tasks.title: Email
tasks.prompt: You recently attended a language course abroad. Write an email to the course director, thanking them for the experience but also pointing out some problems with the accommodation and the social programme.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Ms. Sterling,

I am writing to express my sincere gratitude for the 'Intensive English' course I attended last month at your Brighton centre. The quality of the teaching was exceptional, and I feel my confidence in speaking has improved tremendously.

However, I feel I should bring certain matters to your attention that detracted from the overall experience. Firstly, regarding the accommodation, my host family was very welcoming, but the room provided was inadequate for a student. There was no desk for private study, and the internet connection was extremely unreliable, which made completing my evening assignments quite frustrating.

Furthermore, I was somewhat disappointed with the social programme. While the weekend trip to London was well-organised, many of the weekday evening activities were cancelled at short notice without explanation. As many of us chose this school specifically for the 'immersive' experience, the lack of consistent social opportunities meant we had fewer chances to practice our English outside the classroom than we had anticipated.

I would suggest that for future courses, the school ensures that all host families can provide a suitable study environment. Additionally, a more reliable social calendar would greatly enhance the value of the programme.

Thank you once again for the excellent academic instruction. I hope these comments are taken as constructive feedback.

Kind regards,
Marco Vieri

tasks.title: Review
tasks.prompt: You have seen this announcement in an entertainment magazine: 'We are looking for reviews of documentaries that taught you something new. Tell us about the documentary, why it was interesting, and whether it changed your mind about a certain topic.' Write your review.
tasks.modelAnswer:
An Eye-Opening Journey: 'The Social Dilemma'

Directed by Jeff Orlowski, 'The Social Dilemma' is a thought-provoking documentary that explores the darker side of social media. Combining expert interviews with a fictional narrative, it provides a chilling insight into how tech giants manipulate human behaviour.

The documentary features interviews with former executives from major platforms like Facebook and Google. What makes it so compelling is that these are the very people who built the algorithms they are now warning us about. They explain in layman's terms how features like 'infinite scroll' and push notifications are designed to exploit human psychology for profit, often at the expense of our mental health and political stability.

Before watching this film, I considered myself quite savvy regarding online privacy. However, the documentary profoundly changed my perspective on 'free' services. It made me realise that if I am not paying for the product, I am the product. The revelation about the extent to which our data is used to predict and influence our future actions was truly startling.

Since viewing it, I have significantly reduced my daily screen time and deactivated most of my notifications. I found the documentary to be an essential wake-up call for anyone living in the 21st century.

I would wholeheartedly recommend 'The Social Dilemma'. It is not just an informative film; it is a necessary critique of the tools we use every day. It will certainly make you think twice before picking up your phone.
